title: File System Entry CRUD Operations

steps: Validate entry fields against allowedForCreate (sanitize) and requiredForCreate ['uuid','parent_uid'] (throw if missing)
Instantiate Update op with uuid + sanitized entry (STATUS_PENDING)

enqueue_flow: LOOP [while currentState.queue.length > max_queue OR deferredState.queue.length > max_queue]: await queueSizePromise (backpressure)
GUARD: if op not instanceof BaseOperation → throw Error('Invalid operation')
BRANCH: if status === STATUS_READY → use currentState; else → use deferredState
Register op.uuid in state.updating_uuids[]; push op to state.queue
BRANCH: if entryListeners_[op.uuid] exists → fire all listeners, delete key (notify waiters)
await op.awaitDone() [suspends caller until op.status = STATUS_DONE]
exec_flow: START: checkShouldExec_() → if STATUS_READY AND queue non-empty → exec_()
GUARD: if status !== STATUS_READY → throw 'Duplicate exec_ call'
FOR each op in queue: set op.status = STATUS_RUNNING
FOR each op in queue: set op.status = STATUS_DONE (resolves TeePromise, unblocks awaitDone callers)
Call flipState_(): currentState ← deferredState; reset deferredState; resolve queueSizePromise
Call checkShouldExec_() (process next deferred batch if present)
get_flow: Iterate currentState.queue: for ops matching uuid, call op.apply(answer) to accumulate state
Iterate deferredState.queue: same (apply called twice — possible bug)
BRANCH: if answer.is_diff → fetch base entry from DB via find(NodeUIDSelector(uuid)) then merge: { ...base_entry, ...answer.entry }
Return answer.entry (may be undefined if no queued ops and not fetched)
waitForEntry_flow: Extract NodeUIDSelector from node; if null → return immediately (no selector available)
BRANCH: if uuid already in currentState.updating_uuids OR deferredState.updating_uuids → invoke callback() immediately
Return detach handle { detach() } to allow unsubscription
find_flow: BRANCH NodePathSelector → findByPath(path) [iterates path parts, queries by parent_uid+name; traces if tracer provided]
BRANCH NodeChildSelector → resolve parent recursively via find(selector.parent), then findNameInParent(parentUUID, name), then find(NodeInternalIDSelector(id))

state_model.deferredState: ops enqueued while job running; promoted via flipState_() after batch commit
state_model.updating_uuids: map of uuid → queue positions, used for waitForEntry checks

operation_sql.Update: UPDATE fsentries SET field=? WHERE uuid=? LIMIT 1
operation_sql.Delete: DELETE FROM fsentries WHERE uuid=? LIMIT 1
operation_sql.findByUID: SELECT <defaultProperties> FROM fsentries WHERE uuid=? LIMIT 1
operation_sql.findByID: SELECT <defaultProperties> FROM fsentries WHERE id=? LIMIT 1
operation_sql.findByPath: SELECT ... FROM fsentries WHERE path=? LIMIT 1 (fast path); else walk parts by parent_uid+name
operation_sql.fast_get_descendants: Recursive CTE over fsentries starting at parent_uid

key_relationships: FSEntryController creates Insert/Update/Delete ops and enqueues them
FSEntryController depends_on TeePromise (per-op done promise + queue-size promise)
FSEntryController depends_on selectors: RootNodeSelector, NodeChildSelector, NodeUIDSelector, NodePathSelector, NodeInternalIDSelector
important_notes: Write ops (insert/update/delete) go through a two-state queue with backpressure at max_queue (default 50)
Concurrent writes while STATUS_RUNNING_JOB land in deferredState; promoted after current batch commits
entryListeners_ allows consumers to wait until a specific UUID is enqueued (waitForEntry), fired on enqueue not on DB commit
get() reads pending state from queue before hitting DB (optimistic read), but deferredState.apply is called twice — likely a bug
flipState_() resolves queueSizePromise to unblock any backpressure waiters
db.batch_write executes all ops in a single batch; individual op STATUS_DONE resolves each caller's awaitDone TeePromise
